source.title: Active interoceptive inference and the emotional brain
source.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B

confidence_rationale: Canonical Seth & Friston review of interoceptive inference framework. Establishes that bodily states are regulated by descending predictions from deep generative models -- the architectural commitment that made-feelings / interoceptive-passivity entries depend on. Explicit on the role of precision (uncertainty) and its translation into neuromodulation, with implications for selfhood and emotion. Source quality very high (Phil Trans R Soc B, Friston coauthor). For ARC-058 the relevance is foundational: the framework requires an interoceptive comparator architecture; whether that comparator shares or separates trunks with somatic-discriminative (z_harm_s) channel is the empirical question ARC-058 vs ARC-033 disagree on. The review supports the existence of the interoceptive comparator without forcing one architectural side.
failure_signatures: If REE implements a working interoceptive forward model but observes flat affective comparator output regardless of arousal precision -- that would falsify Seth & Friston's prediction that interoceptive comparator output should be modulated by precision (neuromodulation analog). The made-feelings sub-cluster requires precision-modulated interoceptive comparator output.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Bodily states are regulated by autonomic reflexes that are 'enslaved' by descending predictions from deep generative models of the internal and external milieu. Interoceptive inference reframes emotion as a process of inferring the causes of internal sensory signals. Precision (uncertainty) plays a key role and translates into neuromodulation. The framework has implications for the emotional brain's functional anatomy and for psychiatric conditions ranging from autism to depression to disorders of self-awareness.
mapping.ree_translation: Seth & Friston's framework establishes the architectural commitment that REE inherits when implementing the affective harm stream (z_harm_a) with a forward model. The made-feelings / interoceptive-passivity cluster -- self-generated affective state arriving without authorship tag -- requires an interoceptive comparator that produces a counterfactual-style output for the affective channel (analogous to MECH-095's counterfactual_forward gap on the somatic-discriminative channel). For ARC-058 vs ARC-033: Seth & Friston do not directly adjudicate, but they establish that the interoceptive comparator exists and operates via descending predictions and precision modulation. The shared-trunk vs separate-streams question is downstream of this architectural commitment.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Seth & Friston is a high-level theoretical synthesis. It does not provide direct evidence for or against ARC-058's shared-trunk reading, only for the broader architectural commitment that interoceptive comparators exist. Mapping into REE V3 implementation (which already has z_harm_a in some claims and SD candidates) is principled but draws on multiple downstream papers not in this entry.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review article in Phil Trans R Soc B 2016. Synthesises predictive-coding, active-inference, and interoception literatures. Friston coauthor.

confidence_components.notes: Phil Trans R Soc B + Friston = high source quality. Mapping fidelity moderate-high; the framework supports the architectural commitment underlying ARC-058 / ARC-033 without choosing between them. Transfer risk low for the broad commitment, higher for specific implementation choices in REE V3.
